mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months where INDPRO is above its 6-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A higher unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals weakening labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 5-day Spearman rank correlation between UNRATE changes and SPX returns is positive over any 6-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is at or below 0.25%, it signals effective zero lower bound and aggressive monetary accommodation, which compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of trading days when FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 8% indicates severe labor market slack, dragging on aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day quarter horizon.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8%, the average 60-day SPX return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) compared to periods with UNRATE below 8%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal strong consumer demand and labor market health, boosting corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and PAYEMS have positive monthly changes, the 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 40% of such months.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) rise above their respective 6-month moving averages, it signals a synchronized manufacturing and housing recovery, which lifts corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day quarter horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and HOUST above their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: The average 60-day SPX return is negative or not significantly positive when both proxies exceed their moving averages; or the signal has a hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or below 0.25% and the 12-month CPI inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) is below 2%, it combines extreme monetary accommodation with low inflation, reducing real discount rates and supporting equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy with low inflation
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the average return is not significantly different from zero at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Low housing starts (HOUST) below 700k units indicate a depressed housing market, reflecting weak household formation and construction activity, which drags on GDP and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day quarter horizon.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 700k, the average 60-day SPX return is positive or not significantly negative; or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in a majority of such episodes.
